blockingIssues.description: The rerun fixes non-array JSON duplicate provenance in `session_input_callback`, but it does so by adding a public `provenance` field to `InputItem::{Text, Json}`, which changes the shape of a facade-reexported public enum and creates a downstream-breaking API regression.

`MCPServerManager::connect_all()` still drops the fresh-manager prior active set on the first strict failure when `drop_failed_servers = false`, so Rust remains out of parity with Python's full-active-set restoration semantics for failed servers that stay active.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in `.factory/library/parity.md` that Python's MCP manager begins with the full configured server list active, so strict-failure preservation in `drop_failed_servers = false` must include the fresh-manager first-failure case.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a coding-conventions note that adding doc-hidden or internal-only fields to public Rust enum variants is still a source-breaking API change for downstream construction and pattern matching, so internal bookkeeping must stay out of public enum layouts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `fix-session-input-callback-json-identity-provenance` found that `crates/agents-core/src/items.rs` added a hidden `provenance` field to the publicly re-exported `InputItem::{Text, Json}` variants, forcing constructor and pattern-match changes across 17 files and introducing a new blocking API regression while fixing callback provenance.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared `docs_audit` command still omits the `docs_contract` suite even though the validation contract treats docs portability and README/runtime alignment as part of the docs audit surface.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected again as a duplicate ambiguous services.yaml change: the manifest already exposes `docs_contract` as a separate command, and changing the existing `docs_audit` entry would be a non-additive naming/ownership decision rather than a low-risk factual update for the validator to apply directly.
